findings.title: Virtual attributes bypass the reserved pseudo-slice names and erase owned time
findings.claim: With `--allow-virtual-attribute-slices`, an attribute may target `(gc)` or `(uncollapsible)`. Both renderers then unconditionally remove that attributed slice, producing matched time with no owning row—the exact failure R6-01 intended to prevent.
findings.reproduction: Using one fact `Leaf /app/leaf.py = 10`, an `App` slice, and `--attribute 'name:Leaf,to:(gc)' --allow-virtual-attribute-slices`, both `.venv/bin/clankerprof` and `target/debug/clankerprof-rs` exited 0 and emitted exactly `{"slices":[],"summary":{"matching_pct":100.0,"matching_time_ns":10,"total_time_ns":10},"tool":"clankerprof_slices"}`. Changing only the target to `Virtual` emitted `Virtual` with `time_ns: 10`, proving the `(gc)` result was attributed and then stripped. The opt-in bypass is at `clankerprof/cli.py:362` and `crates/clankerprof-core/src/main.rs:1260`; unconditional stripping is at `clankerprof/render.py:921` and `crates/clankerprof-core/src/slices.rs:316`.

findings.title: Facts v2 accepts an out-of-range primary value index and silently uses value zero
findings.claim: Neither importer verifies that `profile.primary_value_index` indexes each sample's `values`. Both silently fall back to `values[0]`, allowing malformed facts to select and report the wrong metric.
findings.reproduction: I supplied a v2 artifact declaring value types `[samples, cpu]`, `default_sample_type: "cpu"`, `primary_value_index: 99`, and one sample with `values: [1,100]`; its internally consistent summary claimed total 1. Running `slices --facts` through both CLIs exited 0 and reported `{'slice_time': 1, 'summary': {'matching_pct': 100.0, 'matching_time_ns': 1, 'total_time_ns': 1}}` instead of rejecting index 99. `clankerprof/facts.py:288-316` and Rust `facts.rs:251-265,328-340` propagate the unchecked index; the silent fallback is `clankerprof/model.py:60-66` and Rust `model.rs:92-99`. This contradicts the indexed primary-value reconstruction at `docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:141-145`.

findings.title: The Rust regex surface still rejects and misinterprets valid Python-dialect constructs outside the documented exceptions
findings.claim: The published contract explicitly includes Python backreferences and lists only `$`, Unicode case-folding, and Unicode character classes as engine-native exceptions. Rust nevertheless rejects a valid mixed named-capture/numeric-backreference pattern and gives different semantics to a valid conditional pattern.
findings.reproduction: For leaf path `/app/app`, target `T`, and config pattern `regex:(?P<x>/app)\1`, Python exited 0 with category `Hit`, `time_ns: 7`; Rust exited 2 with `{"error": "Invalid regex pattern '(?P<x>/app)\\1': Error compiling regex: Numbered backref/call not allowed because named group was used, use a named backref instead", "ok": false}`. Separately, on `/app/a`, `regex:(?P<x>/app)/a(?(x)|x)` compiled in both: Python emitted `Hit`/7 while Rust emitted `Other`/7, both exit 0. These are new constructs, not any R5 documented `$`/case-fold/Unicode-class edge. Python uses `re.search` at `clankerprof/patterns.py:125-129`; Rust uses `fancy_regex` at `crates/clankerprof-core/src/targets.rs:442-499`.

findings.title: Percentage slice filtering deletes every row when signed matching time is zero
findings.claim: The zero-total percentage contract renders each slice at 0%, but `--by-slice` has an extra nonzero-total guard. Consequently even thresholds that every 0% row satisfies remove all nonzero signed rows.
findings.reproduction: With valid facts A=+10 and B=-10 and matching slices A/B, both CLIs exited 0 without filtering and emitted `A(time_ns=10,pct=0)` and `B(time_ns=-10,pct=0)`. With either `--by-slice=0%` or `--by-slice=-1%`, both exited 0 with `{"slices":[],"matching_time_ns":0}`. Since each rendered percentage is 0, both rows satisfy `0 >= 0` and `0 >= -1`. The faulty guards are `if total and ...` at `clankerprof/render.py:934-938` and `total != 0 && ...` at Rust `slices.rs:321-326`.

findings.title: The zero-total simple-CSV requirement is vacuous: valid signed targets become header-only
findings.claim: For a zero-total target, the simplified noise filter computes every category as 0% and removes every non-`Other` row regardless of its absolute signed cost. The claimed zero-total test only checks for `inf`/`NaN`, so a report containing no accounting evidence passes.
findings.reproduction: Using target T with category Pos=+10 and Neg=-10, both JSON renderers exited 0 with `Pos(time_ns=10,pct=0)`, `Neg(time_ns=-10,pct=0)`, and `total_time_ns=0`. Both `--format simple-csv` commands exited 0 and printed exactly one line: `Parent Function,Category,CPU %,Top 3 Callsites,Top Leaf Functions`. The drop occurs at `clankerprof/render.py:570-576` and Rust `render.rs:320-326`. The covering test at `tests/test_clankerprof.py:6421-6428` asserts only absence of `inf`/`NaN` for simple CSV, despite the complete-accounting contract at `docs/CLANKERPROF_SPEC.md:339-350`.

findings.title: Misspelled scope fields are silently ignored and can disable exclusions
findings.claim: Scope entries are fixed-shape semantic configuration, but neither loader rejects unknown members. A one-character typo in `exclude_descendants` therefore produces a plausible, successful report with the exclusion silently disabled.
findings.reproduction: For one stack `[Block,S]` worth 10 and cost kind `AppWork`, the correct scope field `exclude_descendants: "name_eq:Block"` made both CLIs exit 0 with boundary `Audited` having `samples: 0,total_time_ns: 0`. Changing only the key to `exclude_descendents` made both exit 0 with no warning and `samples: 1,total_time_ns: 10`, bucket `Other`, category `AppWork: 10`. Python iterates known fields without an allowlist at `clankerprof/cli.py:923-980`; Rust does the same at `crates/clankerprof-core/src/scopes.rs:1258-1359`.

findings.title: Empty attribute predicates are accepted as match-all rules
findings.claim: Regular filters reject an empty value, but attribute parsing checks only for the colon. Because name matching is substring-based, `name:` matches every frame and can silently reassign all ownership.
findings.reproduction: With one `Leaf /else/leaf.py = 10`, slices `App: /app/**` and default `Default`, both CLIs normally emitted `[('Default',10)]`. Adding `--attribute 'name:,to:App'` still exited 0 and changed output to `[('App',10)]`. Python accepts the empty value at `clankerprof/cli.py:475-483` and matches it at `clankerprof/slices.py:121-122`; Rust accepts it at `main.rs:1175-1188` and matches it at `slices.rs:695-699`. This contradicts the claimed invalid-attribute coverage in `docs/CLANKERPROF_PARITY.md:132`.

findings.title: Rust does not match the Python reference on zero-padded gzip profiles
findings.claim: The Rust decoder comment claims `MultiGzDecoder` matches Python `gzip.decompress`, but conventional zero padding after a complete gzip member is ignored by Python and rejected by Rust, breaking accepted-input facts parity.
findings.reproduction: I generated a nonempty pprof containing `Leaf /app/leaf.py = 7`, passed `gzip.compress(raw)` followed by 16 zero bytes to each `facts --profile` command, and observed: Python rc=0 with `{"frames":[[1,1,0,1,1,false]],"strings":["Leaf","/app/leaf.py"],"summary":{"empty_sample_count":0,"non_empty_sample_count":1,"sample_count":1,"total_primary_value":7}}`; Rust rc=2 with `{"error": "invalid gzip header", "ok": false}`. Python calls `gzip.decompress` at `clankerprof/proto.py:246-253`; the false-equivalence comment and decoder are at Rust `proto.rs:294-304`.

summary: Eight new findings were reproduced at HEAD d23516d (six major, two minor); none repeats the prior refuted compare trust-model claim. `./bin/dev check` passed, including the Rust test suites. A standalone `cargo test --workspace` could not open `target/debug/.cargo-build-lock` in the read-only audit sandbox (`Operation not permitted`), although those workspace tests passed through the repository check. The deterministic requirement tracker still reports 71/71.
